Corrected Notice Of Motion For Independent Medical Examination Mental-Health Examination
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Independent Medical Examination Mental-Health Examination.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is transferred to be heard on the discovery calendar (Dept. 301) at 9:00 a.m. on the same date.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Corrected Notice Of Motion For Independent Medical Examination Mental-Health Examination
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant, The Roman Catholic Archbishop of San Francisco's Corrected Notice Of Motion For Independent Medical Examination Mental-Health Examination

Defendant's motion compelling plaintiff to submit to a mental examination is unopposed and granted. Good cause for the mental examination is based on plaintiff's response to interrogatories. The examination shall be by Randall B. Smith, Ph.D. on December 27, 2021 commencing at 9:00 am at 655 Redwood Highway, Suite 119, Mill Valley, CA 94941. The defendant did not designate in its moving papers which of the nine referenced possible mental test examinations will be administered. Defendant's examiner may administer no more than three tests which shall be specifically identified in the Order to be entered. The examination may be audiotaped by the plaintiff. All other examination limitations referenced in the motion shall be restated in the Order. The written report of Dr. Smith's examination shall be forwarded to the plaintiff within three days of its receipt per C.C.P. 2032.610. The Court does not rule at this time whether plaintiff may receive copies of the written test questions and answers and reserves jurisdiction to rule on same if test examination questions and answers are requested by plaintiff. See: Carpenter v. Superior Court, 141 Cal.App.4th 249 (2006).

The defendant shall prepare a draft Order setting forth the above rulings forwarding same to the Court via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com in Word format, copy to plaintiff's counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Independent Medical Examination Mental Health Examination
